mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real personal savings and housing starts indicates a synchronized withdrawal of household demand, which compresses corporate revenue growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline simultaneously, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets, an increase in the industrial production index reflects capacity expansion that outpaces wage-driven cost inflation, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive return prediction reverses to negative when INDPRO rises alongside UNRATE falling below 3.5%, or the forward return differential between high and low INDPRO regimes falls below 0.5% over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP signals underlying economic momentum that supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 expands, or the rolling 1-year directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently high to suppress equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days until policy pivots.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 65% of months where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.2 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of nonfarm payrolls and contraction in real savings indicates a wage-driven consumption boom that overheats demand, ultimately triggering inflationary pressures that force rate hikes and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to precede equity drawdowns when PAYEMS and RSAFS move in opposite directions, or the interaction effect's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a decline in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of cooling aggregate demand, which reduces earnings revision risk and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive return prediction reverses when HOUST declines alongside CPIAUCSL falling below 275, or the signal's win rate drops below 48% during periods of elevated inflation over a 3-year test.
